task: Assess a proposed high-signal debugging trajectory dataset schema and initial customer target list for Datalox trajectory export.
step: evaluate trajectory dataset schema and target companies
summary: Reviewed the proposed dataset schema against the repo's canonical Datalox product boundary and current eval/agent company positioning. Key conclusion: the dataset idea is promising as an export layer, but should center reproducibility, provenance, verification, and Datalox skill/note linkage instead of raw reasoning traces.
observations: Repo product definition says Datalox is a knowledge system for agents, with trace inputs promoted into notes and then skills, not a raw trace dump.
Existing trace artifact code captures task, signal, interpretation, action, matched skill, changed files, and outcome, which maps naturally to an export schema but lacks full reproducibility fields.

Current market sources show W&B Weave, LangSmith, Scale Labs, Labelbox, Humanloop, Replit, and Cursor all have adjacent eval/agent/data activity, but Humanloop docs contain conflicting status signals and coding-agent companies likely have stronger internal data advantages.
title: Reviewed the proposed dataset schema against the repo's canonical Datalox product boundary and current eval/agent compa…
signal: User asked whether a proposed schema and customer target plan is sound.
interpretation: Useful direction, but the schema and GTM need tighter boundaries around eval utility, validation evidence, provenance, and buyer segmentation.
recommendedAction: Define a schema_versioned JSONL export with repro, provenance, verification, skill/note linkage, failure attempts, and redaction/license metadata; start with 25 gold reproducible examples before broad outreach.
